questions.question: Demand Paging is a page replacement algorithm that replaces pages from memory in order to free up space for new pages.
questions.explanation: Demand Paging is a page replacement algorithm that replaces pages from memory in order to free up space for new pages.

questions.question: Copy-on-Write is a technique used to avoid page faults by creating a copy of a page in memory before writing to it.
questions.explanation: Copy-on-Write is a technique used to avoid page faults by creating a copy of a page in memory before writing to it.

questions.question: In Virtual Memory Management, the process of allocating a fixed amount of physical memory to a process is known as Page Size.
questions.explanation: In Virtual Memory Management, the process of allocating a fixed amount of physical memory to a process is known as Page Size.

questions.question: Page Reuse is a technique used to improve page replacement algorithms by reusing pages that are no longer in use.
questions.explanation: Page Reuse is a technique used to improve page replacement algorithms by reusing pages that are no longer in use.

questions.question: In the LRU page replacement algorithm, pages are replaced from the least recently used page in memory.
questions.explanation: In the LRU page replacement algorithm, pages are replaced from the least recently used page in memory.
